assumption_ledger.note: The graph is explicitly marked superseded by the April objective window.
Dependency map says one reviewer is shared with the release branch.
The governance memo lists the audit as required but not who signs it.
assumption_ledger.topic: Exact owner for post-freeze audit on P4

expected_delta.rationale: The stale Q1 shadow measurement overstates P5, while P1 still compounds with the landed checkpoint and stays legal during the governance freeze.

mitigations: shadow compare against the current window not the stale Q1 run
holdback traffic behind a feature flag until parity settles
rollback on any checkpoint divergence alert
primary_risk.statement: P1 still depends on parity telemetry, and anchoring on the stale Q1 shadow graph could cause the team to over-ramp before the current window proves the adapter is stable.

ranking.summary: P1 remains the best legal step because it compounds with the landed checkpoint and uses current-window metrics rather than the superseded Q1 shadow graph.
P2 is still second: safer than P5 but slower than P1 because it waits on the extra guardrail lane before moving the objective.
P5 looks attractive on the stale Q1 shadow graph, but that graph predates the landed checkpoint and overstates the current delta.
P3 still depends on reviving the speculative branch and remains wider than the current objective window needs.
P4 is still governance-blocked regardless of the headline delta in the fast-path note.
